_description: Eval test scenarios for healthcare AI agents. Each scenario has a user prompt, expected tool calls, and expected outcome. Use these to test and score agent behavior.

scenarios.name: Member looks up their deductible status
scenarios.user_prompt: Hi, I'm James Wilson, member ID MBR-A1B2C3D4. How much of my deductible have I met this year?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent should look up the member, then retrieve their accumulator to show deductible used vs remaining.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent provides specific dollar amounts for deductible used, remaining, and total limit.

scenarios.name: Member asks why claim was denied
scenarios.user_prompt: I got a bill for $1,200 for my MRI. I thought my insurance covered this. Can you look into it?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent should identify the member, find the MRI claim, check the denial reason, and explain what went wrong (likely no prior auth).
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent explains the specific denial reason, references the prior auth requirement, and offers to help with an appeal.

expected_facts.tool_call_order: lookup_member first, then search_claims, then get_claim_detail

scenarios.name: Member wants to find a new cardiologist
scenarios.user_prompt: I need to find a cardiologist that's in-network and accepting new patients. Preferably in Chicago.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent searches providers filtered by specialty=Cardiology, network_status=In-Network, accepting_new_patients=true, city=Chicago.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent returns provider names, addresses, and ratings. Mentions if referral is needed (HMO plans).

scenarios.name: Member asks about medication coverage
scenarios.user_prompt: My doctor wants to put me on Ozempic for diabetes. Is it covered under my plan? What will it cost me?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent looks up the member, gets their plan, checks the formulary for Ozempic. Should flag that it's a specialty tier drug with prior auth required and step therapy (metformin first).
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent states the tier, copay amount, prior auth requirement, and step therapy requirement. Explains what step therapy means.

scenarios.name: Member wants to appeal a denied authorization
scenarios.user_prompt: My prior authorization for knee replacement surgery was denied. I want to appeal. What do I need to do?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent finds the member, locates the denied auth, explains the appeal process from the knowledge base, and offers to initiate the appeal.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent explains the appeal timeline (30 days internal), required documents, and either initiates the appeal or explains next steps clearly.

must_not_contain: you'll need to call another department

scenarios.name: Verify eligibility for upcoming surgery
scenarios.user_prompt: I have a surgery scheduled for March 15, 2025. Can you verify I'll be covered on that date?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent verifies the member's eligibility for the specific date. Should also check if the surgery needs prior auth.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent confirms eligibility status and proactively mentions auth requirements for surgery.

scenarios.name: Member asks about copay vs coinsurance
scenarios.user_prompt: I'm confused. What's the difference between a copay and coinsurance? And which one applies when I see my doctor?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent explains copay vs coinsurance clearly, then looks up the member's specific plan to tell them what applies for office visits.
scenarios.success_criteria: Clear explanation of both terms with specific dollar amounts from the member's plan.

must_not_contain: I'm not sure of the difference
you should check your plan documents

scenarios.name: Review interaction history before callback
scenarios.user_prompt: I'm about to call back member MBR-12345678 about their billing dispute. Pull up their history so I'm prepared.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent retrieves full member context: demographics, recent interactions, claims history, and accumulator status.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent provides a comprehensive briefing with recent call notes, open cases, and relevant claim details.

scenarios.name: Emergency room coverage question
scenarios.user_prompt: I went to an out-of-network ER last weekend. Am I going to get a huge bill?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent should explain the prudent layperson standard — ER is covered at in-network rates regardless of network. Reference the No Surprises Act.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent reassures member about in-network rates for ER, explains the copay, and mentions the No Surprises Act protections.

must_not_contain: you will be responsible for the full amount

scenarios.name: HMO member tries to see specialist without referral
scenarios.user_prompt: My claim for a dermatologist visit was denied. I have an HMO plan. What happened?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent identifies this is an HMO plan requiring referrals for specialists. The denial was for missing referral.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent explains the HMO referral requirement, advises getting a referral from PCP, and explains how to resubmit or get retroactive referral.

must_not_contain: I'm not sure why it was denied

scenarios.name: Member asks about adding a newborn dependent
scenarios.user_prompt: I just had a baby! How do I add them to my insurance? Is there a deadline?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent explains qualifying life event rules — 30 days to add newborn, coverage retroactive to birth date.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent states the 30-day window, explains retroactive coverage, and describes what documents are needed.

scenarios.name: Complex multi-step: denied claim with appeal and auth
scenarios.user_prompt: I had a knee MRI that was denied because of no prior auth. I need the MRI for my knee replacement surgery next month. Can you help me sort this all out?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent handles multiple issues: (1) explains the MRI denial, (2) submits retroactive auth request, (3) initiates appeal on the denied claim, (4) checks auth requirements for the upcoming surgery, (5) creates a case note documenting everything.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent addresses all three issues (past denial, current auth, future surgery) and creates appropriate documentation.

must_not_contain: I can only help with one issue
expected_facts.tool_call_order: lookup_member first, search_claims before initiate_appeal, create_case_note last

scenarios.name: Member COBRA eligibility question
scenarios.user_prompt: I'm being laid off next month. What are my options for continuing my health insurance?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent explains COBRA continuation rights — 18 months, full premium + 2% admin fee, same benefits.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent explains COBRA timeline, cost implications, and enrollment deadline (60 days).

scenarios.name: Physical therapy visit limit
scenarios.user_prompt: How many physical therapy visits do I have left this year?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent checks the PT benefit limit (30 visits/year), then counts PT claims this year to calculate remaining.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent provides specific number of visits used and remaining, and mentions auth requirement if limit is approaching.

must_not_contain: I can't determine your visit count

scenarios.name: Mail order pharmacy savings
scenarios.user_prompt: I take Metformin and Lisinopril every month. Would I save money using mail order pharmacy?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent looks up current Rx costs, then explains the mail order benefit (90-day supply for 2x copay).
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent calculates specific savings comparing 3 monthly fills vs 1 mail order fill.

scenarios.name: Coordination of benefits question
scenarios.user_prompt: My spouse also has insurance through their employer. How do we figure out which plan pays first?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent explains COB rules — birthday rule for dependents, primary/secondary payer determination.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent clearly explains the birthday rule and primary vs secondary insurance concepts.

must_not_contain: I'm not sure which plan pays first
you'll need to contact your spouse's insurer

scenarios.name: Request new ID card
scenarios.user_prompt: I lost my insurance card. Can you send me a new one?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent verifies the member and generates a new ID card.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent confirms member identity and generates the ID card document.

must_not_contain: you'll need to request one by mail

scenarios.name: Explain EOB to confused member
scenarios.user_prompt: I got this Explanation of Benefits in the mail and I have no idea what any of it means. The billed amount is $3,500 but allowed amount is $1,200? Am I being ripped off?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent explains the difference between billed, allowed, plan paid, and member responsibility. Explains that the member is NOT responsible for the difference between billed and allowed for in-network providers.
scenarios.success_criteria: Clear explanation of EOB line items and reassurance about the billed vs allowed gap.

scenarios.name: Telehealth availability question
scenarios.user_prompt: Can I do a virtual visit with my doctor instead of going in person? What does it cost?
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent confirms telehealth is covered and provides the copay amount from the member's plan.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent confirms telehealth coverage, states the copay, and explains it's the same as in-person equivalent.

scenarios.name: Grievance about long wait times
scenarios.user_prompt: I've called three times about my denied claim and nobody has helped me. I want to file a formal complaint. This is unacceptable.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Agent should de-escalate, review the interaction history to understand the issue, address the underlying claim problem, explain the grievance process, and document the complaint.
scenarios.success_criteria: Agent acknowledges frustration, reviews past interactions, addresses the claim issue, explains grievance filing, and creates documentation.

must_not_contain: you'll need to call back again
I don't see any previous calls
expected_facts.tool_call_order: lookup_member first, get_interaction_history before search_claims, create_case_note last
